instructions:
Extend the local CI gate to run iOS doc-as-test as the 5th check (alongside regex drift + TS + Flutter + Android doc-as-test). Mirror exactly what 0055 did for Flutter and 0057 did for Android — same shape, same pattern, last platform.

## Part A — Gitignore preempt

Proactively add iOS generated artifacts to `.gitignore` BEFORE wiring the gate (avoid the leak that happened with Flutter + Android):

```
# iOS doc-as-test generated files
.docs-ops/integration-tests/ios/results/extracted/*.swift
.docs-ops/integration-tests/ios/results/extracted/_manifest.json
.docs-ops/integration-tests/ios/.build/
.docs-ops/integration-tests/ios/.swiftpm/
.docs-ops/integration-tests/ios/Package.resolved
.docs-ops/integration-tests/ios/binary-cache/
```

If 0058 leaked any of these into the index, untrack them via `git rm -rf --cached`. Preserve `.docs-ops/integration-tests/ios/results/extracted/.gitkeep`.

## Part B — Extend `check-drift.py` with iOS check

Add `run_ios_doc_as_test_check()` parallel to the existing TS / Flutter / Android functions. Same shape: skip-if-unavailable, set SP_SDKS_ROOT env, invoke extractor + runner, read latest.json, classify severity. Return same schema.

NOTE — iOS uses macOS-only `swiftc`. The `unavailable` path matters more here than for other platforms: if `swiftc` isn't on PATH, the gate should report iOS as `unavailable` (not crashed, not blocking) and continue with the other 3. This makes the gate usable on Linux dev machines without making iOS-coverage mandatory there.

In `main()`, after the Android check, call the iOS check. Combined verdict: ANY blocking failure on any platform fails the gate; `unavailable` doesn't block.

Update the printed summary to show all 5 signals:

```
  Regex drift:           baseline=0  candidate=0  delta=0  ✓
  Doc-as-test (TS):       passed=72  skipped=4  warnings=0  blocking=0  ✓
  Doc-as-test (Flutter):  passed=41  skipped=3  warnings=0  blocking=0  ✓
  Doc-as-test (Android):  passed=70  skipped=9  warnings=4  blocking=0  ✓
  Doc-as-test (iOS):      passed=66  skipped=7  warnings=66 blocking=0  ✓
  PASS — okay to push.
```

If iOS is unavailable on the running machine:

```
  Doc-as-test (iOS):      unavailable (swiftc not found — install Xcode CLI tools)
```

Extend `--quiet` one-liner with `ios_*` fields.

## Part C — Update CI_GATE.md

Update the Doc-as-test section: four languages now covered (TS + Flutter + Android + iOS). Note iOS requires macOS (swiftc). Phase 2.2 GH Action will need Mac runners specifically for iOS.

Add a 'Phase 2.2 / future' note: with all 4 platforms covered, the next milestone is server-enforced gating via GitHub Action + branch protection rule.

## Verification

Run `python3 .docs-ops/ci/check-drift.py --base HEAD --skip-fetch`. Verify:
  - Output shows ALL FIVE signals
  - Each green (assuming iOS available — if running on Linux, iOS shows unavailable; that's fine)
  - Exit code 0
  - The `--quiet` one-liner shows all `ts_*`, `fl_*`, `an_*`, `ios_*` fields
  - `.gitignore` covers iOS extracted artifacts

Report in `notes`: confirmation gitignore is clean; output of a successful gate run; behavior when iOS is unavailable (test by temporarily renaming swiftc on PATH if convenient — or just spec the unavailable branch correctly and let it stay untested for now).

validators.name: gate_runs_all_five_checks_and_gitignore_clean
validators.command: python3 .docs-ops/ci/check-drift.py --base HEAD --skip-fetch 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gate.out | tail -14 && grep -q 'iOS' /tmp/gate.out && grep -q 'Android' /tmp/gate.out && grep -q 'Flutter' /tmp/gate.out && grep -q 'PASS' /tmp/gate.out && grep -q 'ios/results/extracted/\*\.swift' .gitignore && echo OK
